title: About — two people, one stubborn opinion

blocks.headline: Started in 2018 by two people who couldn't agree on the right grind.
blocks.subheadline: Six years later we still argue about it most mornings. The customers seem to enjoy the show.

left.heading: How we got here
left.text: Anya was running coffee programs in three different restaurants and getting tired of compromising on bean quality. Theo was a line cook who'd taken a sabbatical to apprentice at a roaster in Trondheim. They met at a wedding in 2016, argued about pour-over technique for four hours, and decided two years later to lease a storefront together.

right.text: The first month, the espresso machine broke twice and they served forty cups a day. The second month, a food blogger walked in and wrote about the Yirgacheffe. By the end of year one they had a line out the door on Saturday mornings and were roasting twice a week to keep up. They've never paid for advertising. They still take Mondays off to roast.

steps.title: Green arrives
steps.description: Sacks land at the back door on Monday morning. We weigh, log, and store them in the sealed room until they're called up for production. Most lots sit for a week or two before they're roasted.

steps.title: Profile development
steps.description: Anya runs sample roasts on the small drum and tastes them blind against the previous lot. The profile is set when the third blind cupping in a row agrees with the second.

steps.title: Production roast
steps.description: Tuesday is roast day. Three batches, six to twelve kilos each, on the 1972 Probat. Theo runs the machine. The room smells like burnt sugar and grass for hours.

steps.title: Rest and ship
steps.description: Beans rest for at least four days before they go on the bar or in the mail. We pack on Wednesday afternoon and the post office picks up on Thursday morning.

members.name: Anya Volkov
members.bio: Worked five years in restaurant coffee programs before starting Roastery. Trained at the SCA. Will judge your grind setting if you ask.

members.name: Theo Bell
members.bio: Spent a year apprenticing at Jacu Coffee Roastery in Trondheim. Makes the hot chocolate. Quiet but funny.

members.name: Mira Okonkwo
members.bio: Joined in year two from a wine bar in the next neighborhood. Has the best palate in the building. Knows every regular's name.

items.title: Direct over labeled
items.description: We'd rather know the producer's name than carry a certification. When we can have both, we do.

items.title: Patience over hustle
items.description: Three lots a week, six-week rotations, four days of rest before a bag leaves the building. Coffee gets better when you slow down.

items.title: The local table
items.description: We exist because of the regulars. The window seat, the Saturday morning crowd, the kids who come in after soccer practice. The cafe is for them first.

blocks.title: Come say hi.
blocks.description: We're easier to find than to explain.
